commit.message:
feat: add sensible defaults for azd env new / init in --no-prompt mode (#7016)

* feat: add sensible defaults for azd env new / init in --no-prompt mode

When --no-prompt is active:
- Auto-generate environment name from working directory basename
  (via CleanName sanitization) when no name is provided
- Auto-select Azure subscription when only one exists; check
  defaults.subscription config first, then fall back to auto-select
- Auto-set new environment as default (skip Confirm prompt)
- Remove hard error requiring --environment with --template

This enables non-interactive CI/CD flows and extensions to use
azd env new / azd init without implementing custom --no-prompt logic.

Fixes #6934

commit.message:
fix: parser falsely rejecting labels starting with R (e.g. return_home)

The register validator was checking ALL args starting with 'R', including
label arguments in jump instructions. Now skips the label position for
JMP/JZ/JNZ/JEQ/JNE/JLT/JGT/CALL and only flags args that look like
register refs (R followed by digits).

Also preloads with 2 colonies (pheromone_forager vs efficient_forager)
as the default startup configuration.

[Repo Assist] Include type constraints in generated API documentation (#1045)

* Include type constraints in generated API documentation

Fixes #591. Type constraints on generic parameters (e.g. 'T : equality,
'T : comparison, 'T :> IComparable) are now extracted from FCS symbols
and displayed in the member tooltip in the 'Constraints:' line.

Changes:
- TypeFormatter.fs: add formatConstraintAsText and formatConstraintsAsText
  using FSharpGenericParameter.Constraints from FCS; supports equality,
  comparison, struct, reference-type, default-ctor, unmanaged, coercesTo,
  enum, delegate, and member constraints
- ApiDocTypes.fs: add 'constraints: string list' field to ApiDocMemberDetails;
  add Constraints member and FormatTypeConstraints to ApiDocMember
- SymbolReader.fs: compute constraints from generic type parameters and
  pass to ApiDocMemberDetails; empty list for union cases, fields, static params
- GenerateHtml.fs: display 'Constraints:' line after 'Type parameters:';
  also fix: type parameters now show independently of modifiers

excluded platform-specific native modules (@parcel/watcher-linux-x64-glibc,
@swc/core-linux-x64-gnu). Regenerated without legacy-peer-deps to include
all platform binaries. CI still uses --legacy-peer-deps for peer dep compat.

commit.message:
Agent: Target State Alignment (#7551)

* Agent: Target State Alignment

* Initial plan

* Enforce formatter/linter boundary: remove GML-domain comment constants from normalizeFormattedOutput

The formatter's post-processing pipeline must not embed knowledge of
specific GML-domain comment strings synthesized by lint rules. The
removed `ensureBlankLineBetweenVertexFormatComments` function stored two
GML-API-specific explanatory comment constants and inserted blank lines
between them when they appeared adjacent in formatted output.

This violates target-state.md §2.1 and §3.2: the formatter is layout-only
and must not make spacing decisions based on the semantic content of
lint-generated comment text. Spacing decisions rooted in GML API semantics
belong exclusively in @gml-modules/lint.

The removed function was also dead code: KEEP_VERTEX_FORMAT_COMMENT_TEXT
read "completed within a function call" but the lint gm2012 fixture
generates "built within a function call", so the pattern never matched.

Adds a boundary-enforcement test to normalize-output-formatter-boundary.test.ts
documenting the enforced contract with accurate lint fixture strings.

Fix shape property evaluation to use composition frame (fixes rocket flame TrimPath at frame 28) (#57)

* Initial plan

* Initial progress tracking

Co-authored-by: cg-zhou <9943470+cg-zhou@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix: evaluate shape properties at composition frame to match lottie-web

lottie-web uses `frameNum = comp.renderedFrame - offsetTime` compared
against `kf.t - offsetTime`, which is mathematically equivalent to
evaluating at `comp.renderedFrame` vs raw `kf.t` values. Our code was
evaluating at `layer_frame = comp_frame - st` against raw `kf.t`, which
is wrong when `st != 0`.

Add `current_comp_frame` field to Player. In render_layer, set it to
`comp_frame` (before applying start_time offset). All shape property
evaluations (TrimPath, Fill, Stroke, GradientFill, GradientStroke,
Rect, Ellipse, Path, Polystar, Repeater, group Transform, Rounding,
Text, and masks) now use `current_comp_frame`.

This fixes the rocket flame at frame 28: TrimPath keyframes at t=21/28
for `e` and t=23/30 for `s` (all in composition time) are now correctly
evaluated, producing a visible flame segment matching lottie-web.

Co-authored-by: cg-zhou <9943470+cg-zhou@users.noreply.github.com>

* Resolve merge conflicts: incorporate main's fill-rule and MergePaths changes

Pull in upstream changes from main (#56):
- FillShape/GradientFillShape gain fill_rule field (from shape r property)
- Player sets fill-rule per-fill-shape rather than from MergePaths mode
- MergePaths is now a no-op (matching lottie-web behavior)
- parser, model, and test files updated from main

Our current_comp_frame changes are preserved on top of these changes.

standalone and 1ES templates with curl downloads from GitHub Releases, verified

time so generated pipelines always fetch the matching release.

- Add .github/workflows/release.yml (triggered on v* tags)
- Update templates/base.yml and templates/1es-base.yml (6 download sites)
- Add {{ compiler_version }} marker replacement in both compilers

Replace manual tag-triggered release with release-please automation:
- On push to main, release-please maintains a Release PR with changelog
  and Cargo.toml version bump (based on conventional commits)
- Merging the Release PR creates the git tag and GitHub Release
- Build job then compiles the binary and uploads assets to the release

checksum, then renames to 'ado-aw'. Fixed in all 6 download blocks
across both templates.

- Add set -euo pipefail to release asset preparation step

commit.message:
fix: redesign release pipeline to create releases atomically with assets

- Remove 'draft: true' from release-please config (draft releases don't
  trigger events and cause duplicate PRs)
- Add 'skip-github-release: true' so Release Please only creates the tag
- Release Please outputs tag_name and calls release.yml via workflow_call
- release.yml builds macOS/Windows assets then creates the GitHub Release
  with all assets attached in a single atomic operation
- Publish step extracts changelog from CHANGELOG.md for release notes
- Cleans up any leftover draft releases before creating the final one
